encounter.location: UI Health Ambulatory TB Clinic, 1801 W Taylor St, Suite 1E, Chicago, IL 60612

labs.name: uric acid
labs.note: mildly elevated, expected to normalize after pyrazinamide discontinuation

labs.name: sputum AFB smear
labs.note: third consecutive negative smear; specimen collected at this visit

screening.name: visual acuity, left eye
screening.note: left eye returned to baseline; no ethambutol-related visual toxicity identified; final visual acuity check as ethambutol is being discontinued

conditions.name: pulmonary tuberculosis, drug-susceptible
conditions.note: sputum culture conversion confirmed, completing 2-month intensive phase, transitioning to 4-month continuation phase; third consecutive negative AFB smear; chest imaging shows continued improvement

medications.name: pyrazinamide 1500 mg oral tablet
medications.note: discontinued — completing 2-month intensive phase, transitioning to continuation phase

medications.name: ethambutol 1200 mg oral tablet
medications.note: discontinued — completing 2-month intensive phase, transitioning to continuation phase

medications.name: rifampin 600 mg oral capsule
medications.sig: 600 mg by mouth once daily, continue through approximately late July 2026

medications.name: isoniazid 300 mg oral tablet
medications.sig: 300 mg by mouth once daily, continue through approximately late July 2026

medications.name: pyridoxine 50 mg oral tablet
medications.reason: peripheral neuropathy prophylaxis with isoniazid therapy

medications.name: albuterol HFA metered-dose inhaler
medications.sig: 1-2 puffs by inhalation as needed for shortness of breath

document_references.description: Dr. Okafor's 10-week follow-up note documenting completion of TB intensive phase, transition to continuation phase, medication changes (discontinuation of pyrazinamide and ethambutol), reassessment of chronic conditions, and DOT schedule modification to three-times-weekly
Educational materials provided regarding continuation phase expectations, reduced medication regimen, DOT schedule change, and importance of treatment adherence

diagnostic_reports.description: Continued interval improvement. Right upper lobe cavitary lesion further decreased to approximately 1.8 cm with thinning walls. Surrounding infiltrate nearly resolved. Hilar lymph nodes normal in size. Impression: resolving pulmonary tuberculosis with good treatment response.
Sputum culture sent for ongoing surveillance at this visit. Results not yet available.
AST and ALT within normal limits. LFTs stable and reassuring. Individual values not enumerated. Labs drawn 2026-03-26, reviewed at this visit.
Hemoglobin 12.4 g/dL noted (anemia resolved), but full CBC component values (WBC, hematocrit, platelets) not individually enumerated. Labs drawn 2026-03-26, reviewed at this visit.
